$comment: D-CSUBSET-ENUM-INT-CONVERSION (FC8) end-to-end: an enum value used as int across the enumerator-value fold, switch dispatch, enum->int widening (call arg + arithmetic in classify), enum==int comparison, postfix ++, compound-assign += (int->enum write-back), the (int)enum cast, AND a PACKED enum struct-field store+load. base = classify(BLUE) = 7; step: RED ++ -> += -> 2; packed: payload(=100) written before the 4-byte enum field tag(=GREEN=1), so an over-wide 8-byte enum store would clobber payload@4 (D-LIR-INT-MEMORY-WIDTH-EXACT for enums — a scalar enum local masks this, the gap the earlier scalar-only corpus missed). return 7 + 2 - 2 + (payload-100) + (int)tag - 1 = 7. A promotion/signedness/fold/memory-width regression in any form flips the exit. The optimizedPipelines arm proves the flow survives Mem2Reg/ConstFold (the baseline arm is the real-codegen memory witness). arm64 runs under qemu; macho on the macos-latest leg.
